title: English Wycliffite Sermons: Sunday Gospels and Festivals
summary: The checked-in afb3713 XML is a local representation of University of Michigan CME item AFB3713.0001.001, an uncorrected 2006 OCR derived from Thomas Arnold's 1869 Select English Works of John Wyclif, volume I. The target is Arnold's bounded 123-sermon selection—fifty-four Sunday-Gospel sermons, thirty-one Commune Sanctorum sermons, and thirty-eight Proprium Sanctorum sermons—from the unified 294-sermon long English Wycliffite cycle, not the whole cycle and not John Wyclif's oeuvre. Arnold printed Bodleian MS Bodley 788 as his continuous base, used selected variants from Cambridge Ii.1.40, Bodleian Laud Misc.314, Wrest Park 11 (now British Library Add MS 40672), and Bodleian Douce 321, checked only three identified passages against Royal 18 B IX and Cotton Claudius D VIII, and described or examined Trinity B.2.17 and B.4.20 without supplying identified apparatus readings from them. The local research cache preserves a complete Arnold scan, a partial Bodley 788 image selection, complete provider sequences for both Trinity manuscripts, and exact institutional catalog and rights evidence; no complete public facsimile was found for the base manuscript or the six other non-Trinity witnesses as of 2026-07-15.
review_notes: Primary transmission is modeled as repository artifact → U-M encoding → historical U-M page-image set → Arnold edition → Bodley 788 base witness, with selected collation edges to B, C, D, and E.
Royal 18 B IX and Cotton Claudius D VIII are supporting authenticity checks for narrowly identified passages, not general textual bases or systematic collations.
Trinity B.2.17 and B.4.20 are supporting witnesses consulted or described by Arnold; their complete IIIF bundles do not imply that their readings entered the edited text.
Modern Hudson–Gradon and Ghosh scholarship informs work identity and attribution but is not represented as an upstream transmission ancestor.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for afb3713
entities.description: The exact XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The mixed-case U-M identifier and lowercase repository filename designate the same item representation.

The file contains a title page, omitted-page milestones for twenty-nine preliminary pages, and OCR text of Arnold's complete printed pp.1–412; it does not encode Arnold's introduction, contents, or other omitted preliminary prose.
The pinned CME root LICENSE is the Unlicense and repeatedly calls its subject software. No path-specific statement expressly applies that grant to inherited U-M XML, transcription, or markup.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AFB3713.0001.001
entities.description: A public TEI-in-Libraries Level 1 electronic text made by uncorrected OCR from page images of Arnold's 1869 volume I. It encodes the title page and full 123-sermon medieval selection while preserving only page milestones for most front matter.

date_statements.label: U-M publication and header creation
date_statements.notes: The header records creation by mrcb2teiutf.xsl on 2006-02-22.

notes: The item-specific editorial declaration says OCR, no correction, no content editing, and linked digital page images.
The historical page-image production layer is described in the XML, but a stable current whole-volume page-image download was not verified.

entities.label: Historical U-M page-image set for AFB3713.0001.001
entities.description: The page-image production layer from which U-M created its 2006 OCR. The XML header describes 454 600 dpi TIFF G4 images and preserves 448 exact TIFF references through Arnold's printed p.412, but a current image endpoint or complete bundle was not verified.
entities.reproduction_format: Historically described as 454 600 dpi TIFF G4 page images
notes: The XML links 448 image filenames, 00000001.tif through 00000448.tif. Target printed pp.1–412 map to sequences 37–448; sequences 1–36 are title or preliminary matter.
The header's 454-image extent exceeds the 448 linked PB elements by six images. Their surfaces and filenames are not represented in the checked XML and remain unresolved.
This historical entity records an evidenced production layer; survival_status unknown means that current retrievability of the TIFF set has not been established.

entities.label: Thomas Arnold's 1869 Select English Works, volume I
alternate_labels: Select English Works of John Wyclif, Volume I
Sermons on the Gospels for Sundays and Festivals
entities.description: Arnold's edition of 123 Middle English sermons in three sections: Evangelia Dominicalia, Commune Sanctorum, and Proprium Sanctorum. Arnold treated them as Wyclif's, but modern scholarship attributes the long cycle more cautiously to anonymous Wycliffite author or authors.

bibliographic.citation: John Wyclif, Select English Works of John Wyclif, edited by Thomas Arnold, vol. I, Sermons on the Gospels for Sundays and Festivals (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1869).
bibliographic.title: Select English Works of John Wyclif

notes: The target medieval text occupies printed pp.1–412: fifty-four Sunday-Gospel sermons on pp.1–163, thirty-one Commune Sanctorum sermons on pp.165–294, and thirty-eight Proprium Sanctorum sermons on pp.295–412.

Arnold calls Bodley 788 his complete and accurate base and says he reproduced its orthography almost exactly while regulating u/v and i/j by sound, retaining thorn and yogh, and supplying capitals, paragraphing, punctuation, marginal analyses, and biblical references.
W. F. Cornish prepared the transcript through printed p.197; William Sorell prepared the remainder. Arnold was responsible for editorial selection, collation, and presentation.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Arnold's 1869 volume I
entities.description: A complete 458-page PDF derivative of the Princeton Theological Seminary Library copy, including covers, title and preliminary matter, the complete 123-sermon text, and terminal matter.
entities.reproduction_format: Scanned page images rendered as PDF with OCR metadata
notes: The target work maps to printed pp.1–412 and PDF pages 41–452.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Bodley 788
entities.description: Arnold's continuous base manuscript for all 123 sermons in the volume-I selection, an English parchment codex of the late fourteenth century. Arnold called it complete and accurate.

holding.notes: The current MARCO record and pinned Bodleian TEI catalog description identify the manuscript.

notes: English manuscript with illuminated capitals.
The cached public image selection covers only fols.1r–5v, not the whole codex.
Arnold's transcription relationship is continuous across the exact 123-sermon selection, unlike his selected apparatus use of other witnesses.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected images of MS Bodley 788
entities.description: A public ten-canvas color IIIF image selection covering fols.1r–5v, including the opening Sunday-Gospel sermons. It is a partial surrogate, not a complete manuscript facsimile.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest and JPEG derivatives at 1800-pixel width

entities.label: Bodleian catalog descriptions of MS Bodley 788
entities.description: The current MARCO page and pinned Bodleian TEI record used for shelfmark, date, support, content, and digital-object discovery.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ii.1.40
entities.description: A manuscript dated around 1400 that preserves the complete fifty-four Sunday-Gospel series as well as the separate fifty-five Sunday-Epistle series. Arnold used selected B readings in the Sunday-Gospel section.

holding.notes: No current public item-level CUL catalog URL or complete public facsimile was verified during this review.

entities.label: IMEP Summary Lists of Middle English Prose, volume 19
entities.description: The official IMEP summary-list PDF used to identify the contents of Cambridge University Library MS Ii.1.40 and its complete Sunday-Epistle and Sunday-Gospel series.

bibliographic.citation: Index of Middle English Prose, Summary Lists of Middle English Prose, volume 19, Cambridge University Library holdings.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc.314
entities.description: A fifteenth-century English parchment manuscript containing Wycliffite sermons and Vae Octuplex. Arnold used selected C variants in the Sunday-Gospel section.

notes: English manuscript containing Wycliffite sermons and Vae Octuplex.

entities.label: Bodleian catalog description of MS Laud Misc.314
entities.description: The pinned Bodleian TEI record used for shelfmark, date, support, contents, and current MARCO identifier.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 40672
entities.description: An English parchment manuscript dated 1400–1440 that contains Sunday Gospels, Commune Sanctorum, and Proprium Sanctorum. Arnold's apparatus explicitly reports three D readings, although the current British Library catalog says the manuscript was lettered D but not collated by Arnold.

notes: Sunday Gospels begin at f.55, Commune Sanctorum at f.107, and Proprium Sanctorum at f.150b.
The D relation is restricted to the three readings actually visible in Arnold's apparatus and does not assert systematic collation.

entities.label: British Library catalog record for Add MS 40672
entities.description: The current institutional catalog JSON used for shelfmark, date, physical extent, contents, historical designation, and the catalog's statement about Arnold's D siglum.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Douce 321
entities.description: An early-fifteenth-century parchment manuscript and important independent witness used for selected E readings across all three Arnold sections. Arnold records missing leaves spanning part of the Proprium section and the absence of Sermon CIV.

notes: Written in double columns.
The cataloged sermon contents include Sunday material, saints' material, and ferial material.

Arnold says two leaves are missing across material corresponding to printed p.302 through a phrase on p.306; Sermon CIV is absent. Those gaps limit E's coverage but do not make the whole witness lost.

entities.label: Bodleian catalog descriptions of MS Douce 321
entities.description: The current MARCO page and pinned Bodleian TEI record used for shelfmark, date, support, extent, and contents.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 18 B IX
entities.description: An English vellum manuscript dated 1400–1440 containing the Sunday homilies, Commune Sanctorum, and Proprium Sanctorum. Arnold consulted it only for identified authenticity checks in Sermons LXIV, LXV, and LXVII.

notes: Homilies begin at f.1, Commune Sanctorum at f.126, and Proprium Sanctorum at f.158.
No blanket collation or continuous source relationship is asserted.

entities.label: British Library catalog record for Royal MS 18 B IX
entities.description: The current institutional catalog JSON used for shelfmark, date, extent, and sermon-section boundaries.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Claudius D VIII, ff.109–310
entities.description: A mid-fifteenth-century imperfect Middle English Gospel commentary in Cotton Claudius D VIII, component ff.109–310. Arnold consulted it only for identified authenticity checks in Sermons LXIV, LXV, and LXVII.

identifiers.value: Cotton MS Claudius D VIII, ff.109–310

holding.shelfmark: Cotton MS Claudius D VIII, ff.109–310

notes: The current catalog calls the Middle English Gospel commentary imperfect.
No blanket collation or continuous source relationship is asserted.

entities.label: British Library catalog record for Cotton Claudius D VIII, ff.109–310
entities.description: The current institutional component-level catalog JSON used for shelfmark, date, folio range, language, genre, and imperfect status.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS B.2.17
entities.description: A fifteenth-century parchment codex containing the three target sermon sections. Arnold described or examined it under siglum K, but no K apparatus readings were identified in the checked edition.

notes: Stamped leather binding.
Written in double columns, 43 lines to a page.

entities.label: Trinity College complete IIIF facsimile of B.2.17
entities.description: The complete 370-canvas public provider sequence, including covers, boards, flyleaves, all 180 folios, and the three target sermon sections. No separately labeled spine or edge views are present.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest with 1800-pixel JPEG derivatives

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS B.4.20
entities.description: A late-fourteenth- or early-fifteenth-century parchment codex containing an imperfect and discontinuous copy of the three target sermon sections. Arnold described or examined it under siglum M, but no M apparatus readings were identified in the checked edition.

date_statements.label: institutional dating
date_statements.value: late fourteenth or early fifteenth century

notes: Written by two hands.
A lost quire omits sermons between 18 and 36; Vae Octuplex interrupts the sermon-cycle material on ff.47v–50r.

entities.label: Trinity College complete IIIF facsimile of B.4.20
entities.description: The complete 180-canvas public provider sequence, including covers, boards, flyleaves, all 80 folios, the imperfect target sermon sections, and intervening non-target Vae Octuplex. No separately labeled spine or edge views are present.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest with 1800-pixel JPEG derivatives

bibliographic.citation: Index of Middle English Prose, Summary Lists of Middle English Prose, volume 11, Trinity College Cambridge holdings.

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A
entities.description: Official guidance on duration and renewal used to support the U.S. public-domain assessment for Arnold's 1869 edition; it is legal-reference evidence, not a textual source for the sermons.

bibliographic.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.

object.description: Complete Arnold volume I
The corresponding Sunday-Gospel, Commune Sanctorum, and Proprium Sanctorum sermons in Bodley 788

scope.notes: Bodley 788 is the continuous base. Arnold's supplied punctuation, capitalization, paragraphing, references, and regulated letter usage mean the edition is not a diplomatic facsimile.

subject.description: Apparatus readings in the Sunday-Gospel section

object.description: Corresponding Sunday-Gospel readings in CUL Ii.1.40

scope.notes: The relation records selected B variants only, not a continuous source or systematic full-cycle collation.

subject.description: Apparatus readings in the Sunday-Gospel section

object.description: Corresponding Sunday-Gospel readings in Laud Misc.314

scope.notes: The relation records selected C variants only.

locators: p.11, Sermon IV: 'voydid but sadly replenysched, D.'

object.description: The corresponding three readings in Add MS 40672

scope.notes: The present catalog says the manuscript was lettered D but not collated by Arnold, while Arnold's printed apparatus explicitly attributes these three readings to D. The edge is therefore limited to the visible evidence and does not imply systematic collation.

subject.description: Selected E apparatus readings across all three sermon sections

object.description: Corresponding Douce 321 readings, where the witness survives
locators: two missing leaves across Arnold p.302 to a phrase on p.306

scope.notes: The edge records selected variants, not continuous dependence. Explicit lacunae prevent treating E as complete for every target passage.

rights.component: Pinned CME repository XML serialization, including contributor-owned repository additions and inherited U-M transcription and markup
rights.jurisdiction: Repository license assertion; inherited-source scope unresolved
rights.basis: The pinned CME root LICENSE is the Unlicense and broadly permits use of 'this software.' It does not expressly identify the imported U-M XML corpus, data, transcription, encoding, or markup as covered software, and no path-specific notice was found. The exact assertion is retained without treating it as blanket permission for inherited material or as overriding upstream terms.
notes: U-M's current No Copyright–United States assertion is recorded separately for the U-M encoding and does not automatically settle rights in repository additions.
The Unlicense's software wording creates scope uncertainty; this manifest does not convert it into a corpus-wide data license.

rights.component: U-M OCR text, markup, and public item presentation
rights.basis: U-M's current CME collection presentation marks its materials No Copyright–United States. The older XML also carries generic language saying copyright or distribution restrictions may apply; both statements are retained.
notes: No Copyright–United States is jurisdiction-specific and not a worldwide license.
Confirm item-specific bulk-reuse terms with U-M if redistribution of the encoding rather than newly derived research is planned.

rights.component: Historical U-M TIFF page-image production set for Arnold's 1869 volume
rights.basis: U-M's current CME collection marks its materials No Copyright–United States, and the underlying 1869 volume is public domain in the United States. The older XML's generic restriction warning and the current TIFF delivery status remain unresolved.
notes: No Copyright–United States is jurisdiction-specific and not a worldwide license.
Ask U-M to confirm the TIFF-specific scope and current delivery terms before bulk redistribution.

rights.component: Arnold's 1869 edited text, introduction, notes, apparatus, and arrangement
rights.basis: The volume was first published in 1869, well before the current U.S. public-domain cutoff for published works.
notes: This U.S. conclusion does not itself determine every non-U.S. jurisdiction or rights in modern scans.

rights.component: Internet Archive PDF derivative, scan images, and scan-layer metadata
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The underlying 1869 edition is public domain in the United States, and Wikimedia Commons marks the exact Internet Archive-derived file with the Public Domain Mark. The consulted Internet Archive metadata contains no item-specific license field, so provider-file reuse is not overstated as an affirmative open license.
notes: The Public Domain Mark is a status label, not a license.
Preserve attribution and provenance even where copyright permission is not required.

rights.component: Underlying medieval sermon text represented by the physical witness
rights.basis: The text predates modern copyright by centuries. Physical ownership, repository access, catalog descriptions, and modern manuscript photographs remain separate layers.
notes: This assessment does not grant access to the physical manuscript or reuse rights in any modern image.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian manuscript photographs, IIIF metadata, and cached ten-image bundle
rights.basis: The Digital Bodleian object and terms apply Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 to the images. Commercial or print-on-demand redistribution is outside the noncommercial license unless separate authorization is obtained.
notes: The gitignored bundle is a research cache and should not be committed or redistributed commercially without permission.

rights.component: Modern institutional catalog text, metadata serialization, and website presentation
rights.basis: No item-specific open license covering the complete modern catalog description was identified. Public access is not treated as permission to redistribute the provider's prose or presentation.

editorial_practices.representation: Title-page transcription, twenty-nine omitted preliminary-page milestones, and OCR text of printed pp.1–412
editorial_practices.description: The electronic item retains the title page and full medieval sermon selection but omits most introductory and contents prose while preserving its pagination as PB milestones.
notes: The header separately identifies the process as uncorrected OCR; OCR is not an available editorial-practice enum.

editorial_practices.representation: Volume-I selection of 123 sermons in three sections
editorial_practices.description: Arnold's volume I prints the fifty-four Sunday-Gospel sermons, thirty-one Commune Sanctorum sermons, and thirty-eight Proprium Sanctorum sermons, not the complete 294-sermon cycle.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Bodley 788 lacked systematic capitals and paragraphing according to Arnold
editorial_practices.description: Arnold supplied capitals and paragraphing throughout the edited text rather than reproducing those visual features diplomatically.
notes: Arnold separately retained thorn and yogh and regulated u/v and i/j by sound; those letter practices have no dedicated schema enum and remain documented in entity notes.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Bodley 788 had no punctuation according to Arnold
editorial_practices.description: Arnold supplied punctuation throughout the edited text.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Variant readings in witnesses B, C, D, and E, plus narrow passage checks in G and I
editorial_practices.representation: Selected footnote apparatus and authenticity notes
editorial_practices.description: Arnold used selected readings rather than constructing the edition from an exhaustive full-witness collation. B and C are confined to Sunday-Gospel variants; D is represented by three identified apparatus readings; E contributes selected variants across the three sections subject to lacunae; G and I support only three identified passage checks and are not modeled as collated sources.
editorial_practices.scope: selected apparatus readings and checks; exact witness scopes are recorded in relations
notes: K and M were described or examined, but no K or M apparatus readings were identified in the checked edition.

evidence.citation: General Corpus / CME source XML, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/afb3713.xml, at CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: HEADER; FRONT; BODY DIV1 nodes AFB3713.0001.001:3–5
evidence.summary: Primary local evidence for AFB3713.0001.001 identity, 2006 uncorrected OCR production, Arnold's 1869 source volume, title page, omitted preliminaries, three sermon sections, page milestones, apparatus, and language.

evidence.citation: Structural, boundary, and apparatus inspection of afb3713.xml with XPath/element counts, page and section review, repository hashing, and git-object identification.
evidence.locator: BODY nodes :3–5; edition-note verse markup; 123 DIV2 sermon nodes
evidence.summary: Confirms five DIV1 elements, 123 DIV2 sermons, 252 headings, 499 paragraphs, 448 linked page breaks against the header's 454-image extent, 798 NOTE1 elements plus three header notes, five verse groups and twenty-six verse lines all confined to Arnold's modern notes, exact 54/31/38 section counts, printed pp.1–412 coverage, 1,052,152-byte size, git blob, and SHA-256.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME root LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c (The Unlicense).
evidence.summary: The 1,211-byte file permits copying, modification, publication, sale, and distribution of 'this software' and dedicates copyright interests in 'this software.' It does not expressly identify inherited U-M XML, data, transcription, encoding, or markup.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, item AFB3713.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Persistent public discovery route for the U-M digital text and linked page context. Automated retrieval met a Cloudflare challenge on the last check; the source packet preserves the same item identifier and production statement.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection information and rights statement.
evidence.summary: Current collection-level evidence that CME materials are publicly accessible and marked No Copyright–United States, retained alongside the older generic restriction language in the item XML.

evidence.citation: John Wyclif, Select English Works of John Wyclif, ed. Thomas Arnold, vol. I, Sermons on the Gospels for Sundays and Festivals (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1869), complete Internet Archive scan.
evidence.locator: title page PDF p.7; contents pp.xxiii–xxix; introduction pp.i–xxii; text printed pp.1–412 / PDF pp.41–452
evidence.summary: Complete immediate-edition evidence for title, publication, exact three-section selection, printed pp.1–412, sermon numbering, textual content, notes, apparatus, and PDF mapping.

evidence.citation: Thomas Arnold, introduction and manuscript account in Select English Works of John Wyclif, vol. I (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1869), pp.i–xxii.
evidence.locator: especially pp.ix–x and xiii–xxii; apparatus pp.11 and 17
evidence.summary: Editor-authored evidence for Bodley 788 as the continuous base, manuscript sigla and use, selected variants and passage checks, exact D apparatus readings, Douce lacunae, orthographic policy, supplied capitalization and punctuation, editorial apparatus, and Cornish/Sorell transcription responsibilities.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata API response for selectenglishworks01wycl.
evidence.summary: Supplies exact public filenames, item identity, 2008-07-14 public date, Princeton Theological Seminary Library contributor, and derivative inventory. No licenseurl or rights field was found.

evidence.citation: Wikimedia Commons file page for the Internet Archive-derived Select English Works of John Wyclif volume-I PDF.
evidence.summary: The exact scan-derived file page applies the Public Domain Mark and records Internet Archive provenance; this is retained separately from the Internet Archive metadata, which provides no explicit license field.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries medieval manuscripts TEI catalog record for MS Bodley 788, pinned at repository commit cd36dce9a104617f960dab2c97ea78f95f917496.
evidence.summary: Institutional evidence for shelfmark, late-fourteenth-century date, parchment support, English origin, contents, decoration, and identifiers.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries MARCO record for MS Bodley 788.
evidence.summary: Current institutional discovery page for the physical manuscript and its public Digital Bodleian image selection.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF Presentation manifest for selected folios of MS Bodley 788.
evidence.summary: Defines the ten-canvas public selection, exact image services, folio labels 1r–5v, Digital Bodleian object route, Bodleian attribution, and CC BY-NC 4.0 image license.

evidence.citation: Direct archive, inventory, and boundary inspection of the cached Bodley 788 selected-image bundle.
evidence.summary: Verifies ZIP integrity, ten inventoried images plus manifest and inventory, fols.1r–5v labels, exact first and last Image API URLs, and presence of the opening target sermons. The bundle is explicitly partial relative to the manuscript.

evidence.citation: Index of Middle English Prose, Summary Lists of Middle English Prose, volume 19, Cambridge University Library manuscripts.
evidence.summary: The Ii.1.40 entry identifies the manuscript's complete fifty-five Sunday-Epistle and fifty-four Sunday-Gospel series; the cached complete 20-page PDF is metadata-only.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries medieval manuscripts TEI catalog record for MS Laud Misc.314, pinned at repository commit cd36dce9a104617f960dab2c97ea78f95f917496.
evidence.summary: Institutional evidence for shelfmark, fifteenth-century date, English parchment codex, Wycliffite sermon contents, Vae Octuplex, and MARCO identifier.

evidence.citation: British Library catalog record 032-002092485 for Add MS 40672, formerly Wrest Park MS 11.
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, 1400–1440 dating, vellum extent, the three sermon-section folio starts, lack of digitized content, and the statement that Arnold lettered it D but did not collate it.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries medieval manuscripts TEI catalog record for MS Douce 321, pinned at repository commit cd36dce9a104617f960dab2c97ea78f95f917496.
evidence.summary: Institutional evidence for shelfmark, early-fifteenth-century date, parchment support, extent, and sermon contents.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries MARCO record for MS Douce 321.
evidence.summary: Current institutional record for shelfmark, date, support, extent, contents, and online-status information.

evidence.citation: British Library catalog record 040-002100589 for Royal MS 18 B IX.
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, 1400–1440 dating, vellum extent, homily/Commune/Proprium section starts, and absence of digitized-content delivery.

evidence.citation: British Library catalog record 040-002114236 for Cotton MS Claudius D VIII, ff.109–310.
evidence.summary: Confirms the component shelfmark and folio range, 1430–1470 dating, Middle English Gospel commentary, imperfect status, and absence of digitized-content delivery.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge online manuscript catalog, B.2.17.
evidence.summary: Confirms the fifteenth-century parchment codex, 180-folio extent, dimensions, binding and layout, sermon contents, and complete-IIIF discovery route.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge IIIF Presentation manifest for B.2.17.
evidence.locator: 370 canvases; f.1r canvas 7; f.43v canvas 92; f.46r canvas 97; f.82v canvas 170; f.112v canvas 230
evidence.summary: Defines all 370 provider canvases, exact image services and labels, target mapping to canvases 7–92, 97–170, and 170–230, Trinity attribution, and CC BY-NC 4.0.

evidence.citation: Direct archive, inventory, and target-boundary inspection of the cached complete B.2.17 IIIF bundle.
evidence.summary: Verifies ZIP integrity, all 370 inventoried images plus manifest and inventory, complete provider sequence, target ranges, non-target gap ff.44r–45v, shared f.82v boundary, and exact Image API endpoints.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge online manuscript catalog, B.4.20.
evidence.summary: Confirms the late-fourteenth- or early-fifteenth-century parchment codex, 80-folio extent, dimensions, two hands, imperfect sermon cycle, lost quire, Vae Octuplex interruption, and IIIF route.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge IIIF Presentation manifest for B.4.20.
evidence.locator: 180 canvases; f.16r canvas 43; f.47r canvas 105; ff.47v–50r canvases 106–111; f.50v canvas 112; f.80r canvas 171
evidence.summary: Defines all 180 provider canvases, exact image services and labels, target mapping to canvases 43–105 and 112–171, intervening Vae Octuplex canvases 106–111, Trinity attribution, and CC BY-NC 4.0.

evidence.citation: Direct archive, inventory, and target-boundary inspection of the cached complete B.4.20 IIIF bundle.
evidence.summary: Verifies ZIP integrity, all 180 inventoried images plus manifest and inventory, complete provider sequence, the two target runs, the non-target Vae Octuplex interruption, and exact Image API endpoints.

evidence.citation: Index of Middle English Prose, Summary Lists of Middle English Prose, volume 11, Trinity College Cambridge manuscripts.
evidence.locator: PDF p.1, B.2.17 and B.4.20 entries
evidence.summary: Supporting institutional index evidence for B.2.17 and B.4.20. The cached complete PDF is metadata-only and contains no target transcription or facsimile.

evidence.citation: Middle English Compendium bibliography entry BIB3873, English Wycliffite Sermons, ed. Anne Hudson and Pamela Gradon, five volumes (1983–1996).
evidence.summary: Current bibliography and witness-census evidence for the modern edition, surviving manuscript shelfmarks, approximate witness dates, and microfilm references.

evidence.citation: Anne Hudson and Pamela Gradon, eds., English Wycliffite Sermons, vol. 1 (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1983).
evidence.locator: bibliographic description and publisher synopsis; ISBN 0198127049 / 9780198127048
evidence.summary: Modern-edition evidence that followers of Wyclif produced the complete long cycle and that volume I covers dominical Gospel and Epistle sermons; used for work identity and cautious attribution, not modeled as an upstream source of Arnold or the CME artifact.

evidence.citation: Kantik Ghosh, 'The English Wycliffite Sermons: Thinking in Alternatives?', in Wycliffite Heresy: Authority and the Interpretation of Texts (Cambridge University Press, 2001), DOI 10.1017/CBO9780511483288.005.
evidence.summary: Scholarly framing of the 294-sermon long cycle as unified while single or multiple authorship remains obscure and collaborative production is plausible.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian terms of use and image licensing guidance.
evidence.summary: Applies CC BY-NC 4.0 to Digital Bodleian images, identifies noncommercial-use conditions, and supplies imaging@bodleian.ox.ac.uk for commercial licensing or reproduction enquiries.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Ordering Images.
evidence.summary: Provides the current online Imaging Services order route, photo@lib.cam.ac.uk contact, private-study versus other-use distinction, and minimum-charge information.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Terms and Conditions of Image Supply and Reproduction.
evidence.summary: States that supplied images may not be reproduced for any reason without prior written permission and governs intended publication or commercial use separately from image supply.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services.
evidence.summary: Current route for ordering digital reference or reproduction images by shelfmark/title/folio and for contacting licensing about reuse.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, Wren Digital Library photography, image supply, and licensing guidance.
evidence.summary: Says already digitized images may be downloaded for personal study, asks researchers to seek permission for publication, supplies wren.library@trin.cam.ac.uk, and says prices vary by use; each IIIF manifest separately applies CC BY-NC 4.0.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.summary: Official guidance used to assess the U.S. public-domain status of Arnold's 1869 publication. The complete official PDF is cached as supporting legal evidence and contains no target text.

evidence.citation: 17 U.S.C. §105, Subject matter of copyright: United States Government works.
evidence.summary: Provides the statutory basis for treating the official Copyright Office circular, as a federal-government work, as outside U.S. copyright protection.

open_questions.question: What single-author, collaborative, compilatory, or revision model best explains the unified 294-sermon long cycle, and what relationship did John Wyclif have to its composition?
next_steps: Consult the complete Hudson–Gradon five-volume edition and later scholarship rather than using Arnold's attribution as the critical baseline.
Separate evidence for cycle unity from evidence for one author, several authors, revision, or compilation.
Retain manuscript and edition attributions as reception evidence without converting them into confirmed authorship.

open_questions.question: Can the exact 123-sermon selection or its three constituent sections be dated more narrowly without depending on Wyclif's personal authorship or transferring dates from surviving copies?
next_steps: Review dating arguments across all five Hudson–Gradon volumes and later textual scholarship.
Reassess Arnold's Richard II and persecution evidence independently of his authorship conclusion.
Keep witness production dates distinct from composition.

open_questions.question: How should Arnold's three explicit D apparatus readings be reconciled with the current British Library statement that Wrest Park 11 / Add MS 40672 was lettered D but not collated by Arnold?
next_steps: Ask the British Library whether the catalog statement means not systematically collated rather than never consulted.
Inspect Arnold's surviving papers, collations, or proof materials if identified.
Retain the lineage edge at the three printed readings only unless stronger evidence appears.
notes: The conflict does not justify deleting the printed D readings or expanding them into a systematic collation.

open_questions.question: Does Bodleian Manuscript Photographs microfilm 177(2), cited by MEC for Douce 321, cover the entire manuscript, and how can it currently be accessed or ordered?
next_steps: Ask Bodleian Imaging Services to confirm microfilm 177(2), its exact folio coverage, format, and current delivery options.
Prefer a new whole-codex color facsimile if available, including binding, endleaves, blanks, and all folios.
Do not model the unverified microfilm as an extant facsimile entity until coverage and access are confirmed.

open_questions.question: Are complete public digital facsimiles available for Bodley 788, CUL Ii.1.40, Laud Misc.314, Add MS 40672, Douce 321, Royal 18 B IX, or Cotton Claudius D VIII beyond the sources checked here?
next_steps: Ask each holding institution whether an unindexed or reading-room surrogate exists.
For Bodley 788, distinguish the verified ten-image fols.1r–5v selection from a whole-codex facsimile.
If ordering images, request the whole manuscript where practical and retain an exact work-portion map inside the full object.
Update this dated negative finding if a public IIIF manifest, complete viewer, or downloadable surrogate is found.

open_questions.question: What exact page-image set and digitization workflow produced U-M item AFB3713.0001.001, and is a stable whole-volume image download still available?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections whether the historical linked images remain retrievable and whether they derive from the same physical copy as the Internet Archive scan.
Compare image identifiers and page geometry if a current image route becomes available.
Keep the encoding-to-Arnold relation independent of an unverified current U-M facsimile entity.
